prompt: Design a Unified Campus Operations & Logistics Management Portal to coordinate offline events, fulfillment, and accountability within a single on-premise site. The interface, built with Layui, provides role-based navigation for Administrators, Operations Staff, Team Leads, Reviewers, and Regular Users. Regular Users can browse and sign up for activities with clear rules shown at publish time (signup window, max headcount, eligibility tags, and required supplies); once an activity is Published, edits create a new version and users see a highlighted change log, while state transitions follow Draft, Published, In Progress, Completed, and Archived with visible timestamps in MM/DD/YYYY and 12-hour time. Operations Staff can create orders tied to an activity (e.g., equipment rental or printed materials) and track them from placement through ticketing and closure, while Team Leads can manage task breakdowns, staffing counts, and checklists. The portal includes two complementary search experiences: a global full-text search with highlighting across titles, body text, authors, and tags, plus a logistics/order search with tokenization, optional synonym and pinyin matching for names, and basic spell correction; both support multi-dimensional filters and sorting by recency, popularity, reply count, and relevance. Recommendations appear on list pages and detail pages using local behavior signals (views, saves, signups, and tags), with cold-start defaults based on top-performing tags in the last 30 days, deduplication to avoid repeating the same activity/order family, and diversity rules that cap any single tag to 40% of a feed page. Managers can also build custom dashboards via drag-and-drop widgets, drill into linked charts, favorite common views, and export snapshots to PNG, PDF, or Excel for offline sharing. The backend uses ThinkPHP to expose REST-style endpoints consumed by the Layui frontend, with MySQL persisting all core records and audit trails. Order lifecycle is enforced by a state machine with strict transitions: Placed, Pending Payment, Paid, Ticketing, Ticketed, Canceled, and Closed; Pending Payment auto-cancels after 30 minutes, Paid orders can be refunded only by an Administrator before Ticketed, and Closed records are immutable except for invoice address corrections with reviewer approval. Fulfillment supports shipment creation with package splitting (one order to multiple packages), carrier name and tracking number entry, offline tracking lookups against locally entered scan events, delivery confirmation, exception receipts, arrival reminders shown in-app, and optional local subscription preferences that only affect on-screen alerts. The violation/demerit system stores configurable rules with point values (e.g., +5 reward for on-time completion, -10 for missed shift), allows evidence attachments (JPG/PNG/PDF up to 10 MB each with file-type validation and SHA-256 fingerprinting), and runs an appeal and re-review workflow with required decision notes; points auto-aggregate per individual and per group, triggering alerts at 25 points (manager review) and 50 points (administrative action). Security is fully offline: login uses local username and password with minimum 10 characters, lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes, salted hashing, and role-based access control; sensitive fields such as passenger identifiers or invoice contacts are masked in UI and encrypted at rest, and all exports are watermarked with username and timestamp to reduce leakage. Incremental indexing updates run locally on create/update/delete events with a nightly cleanup job that removes orphaned index entries older than 7 days to keep search results accurate without any external services.
